data.narrative:
Nara Space Technology is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised approximately $25M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-stage, same-sector companies: Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B), Aalyria ($1.3B), and Hubble Network ($400M).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach, given that target revenue remains undisclosed—a common constraint in private company analysis that precludes public-comp P/S anchoring. The three private comparables yield a median valuation of $1.3B, with a range spanning $400M to $2.0B. A public-comp universe of six Space & Aerospace equities (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trades at a median 64.2x P/S, but cannot be applied without revenue data.

The analysis supports a triangulated valuation range of $1.1B to $1.5B, centered at $1.3B. This range reflects the interquartile spread of the peer set. Key uncertainties include the target's revenue trajectory, technology differentiation relative to peers, and customer concentration—factors not quantified in available data. The single-method reliance on peer median implies moderate confidence; expansion of the private peer set or disclosure of near-term revenue guidance would strengthen conviction in the valuation midpoint.
